titleStmt.title: SII 22.47: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao

No. 47. (A.R. No. 47 of 1906) TANJAVUR DISTRICT, MAYURAM TALUK, TIRUKKAḌAIYŪR. AMṚITAGHAṬĒŚVARA TEMPLE—ON THE WALL OF THE THIRD GŌPURA. KṚISHNADĒVARĀYA-Vijaya, Āḍi 20 (A.D. 1533—34) [or Vṛisha, A.D. 1521, July 18]

summary.p: This is a pair of verses in Tamil in praise of one Āpatsahāyaṉ Abhaṅguraṉ who is called ‘the chief of Kaḍavai’ and who is stated to have taken part in the invasion of Raichur and Bījāpūr (Vijayānagara) along with the king. The inscription records the renovation of the temple and the institution of the daily recital of the Vēdas by suitable provision of lands.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śubhamastu . viṣaiya varuṣam Āṭi-mātam 2 10 tiyati maruvalar poṟṟi varuṅ kiṟuṣṇarāyaṉ maṉam makiḻa parivuṭaṉ yirāccūr vijaiyā nakaramun taṇṭu paṇ
ṇit tiruvaḷar colaik kaṭavūrar tevatāṉam Uṇṭākkivittāṉ Arumaṟaiyoṉ yiyal Āpaṟcakāyaṉ Apaṅkuraṉeṉ .
ceṟṟalāreṇ kiṟuṣṇarāyaṉ muṉṉe tirukkoyilkaḷil coṟpayi lākama niti yellān turaikaḷ mecca niṟṟalum collit tiruppaṇi
vilai Iṭṭava ṉamaṟpuyaṉ Āpaṟcakāyaṉ kaṭavaikku maṉṉavaṉe .
Digital edition of SII 22.47 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
